understanding.goal_understanding: The objective in each level was to place every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked '+' in the semantic grid, with the win condition requiring all goal tiles to be occupied simultaneously (so multi-goal levels needed multiple selectables correctly positioned at the same time). Completing all levels would finish the full KA59 puzzle run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used cardinal MOVE actions that shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) per step unless blocked; collisions with walls or other blocking objects could prevent movement, while some adjacent objects (blocks) were pushable and would move along if pushed. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces, and control could be switched by issuing a CLICK action targeting the exact pixel/grid coordinates of another selectable as listed in the state; the semantic grid provided a cell-level view (P/S/B/#/+/.) while actual actions depended on the pixel coordinates shown for player and objects.
